header_comments: Feishu emoji types for typing indicator
inline_comments: Feishu API error codes that indicate the caller should back off. These must propagate to the typing circuit breaker so the keepalive loop can trip and stop retrying. - 99991400: Rate limit (too many requests per second) - 99991403: Monthly API call quota exceeded - 429: Standard HTTP 429 returned as a Feishu SDK error code @see https://open.feishu.cn/document/server-docs/api-call-guide/generic-error-code

Custom error class for Feishu backoff conditions detected from non-throwing SDK responses. Carries a numeric `.code` so that `isFeishuBackoffError()` recognises it when the error is caught downstream.

Check whether an error represents a rate-limit or quota-exceeded condition from the Feishu API that should stop the typing keepalive loop. Handles two shapes: 1. AxiosError with `response.status` and `response.data.code` 2. Feishu SDK error with a top-level `code` property

Check whether a Feishu SDK response object contains a backoff error code. The Feishu SDK sometimes returns a normal response (no throw) with an API-level error code in the response body. This must be detected so the circuit breaker can trip. See codex review on #28157.

Add a typing indicator (reaction) to a message. Rate-limit and quota errors are re-thrown so the circuit breaker in `createTypingCallbacks` (typing-start-guard) can trip and stop the keepalive loop. See #28062. Also checks for backoff codes in non-throwing SDK responses (#28157).
Feishu SDK may return a normal response with an API-level error code
instead of throwing. Detect backoff codes and throw to trip the breaker.
Silently fail for other non-critical errors (e.g. message deleted, permission issues)
Remove a typing indicator (reaction) from a message. Rate-limit and quota errors are re-thrown for the same reason as above.
Check for backoff codes in non-throwing SDK responses
Silently fail for other non-critical errors
